personalInfo.name: Leonel E. Nieto
personalInfo.tagline: Executive Leader — Data Strategy | Analytics Transformation | Digital Innovation
personalInfo.heroIntro: I help organizations make better decisions, build stronger systems, and turn ideas into action. My work brings together strategy, data, planning, and people-centered change.
personalInfo.heroNote: Rooted in higher education. Built for any field where leaders need clarity, focus, and progress.
personalInfo.summary: Strategic analytics executive with over a decade of experience leading data strategy, enterprise BI, digital infrastructure, and performance systems. Proven track record of modernizing analytics ecosystems, mentoring cross-functional teams, and translating complex data into impactful insights for executive and operational decision-making.

stats.label: Core disciplines: strategy, data, systems, people

aboutBody: I'm a strategy and data leader who helps organizations move from complexity to clarity. I work best where ideas, people, data, and systems need to come together — and where the stakes are real.

I've led work across analytics infrastructure, enterprise BI, strategic planning, governance, organizational change, and executive decision support. While my experience is rooted in higher education, the way I work is not limited to one field.
I help teams understand what matters, focus their effort, and build systems that make progress easier to see and sustain.
personalInfo.pullQuote: My job is to help people make sense of messy problems and move important work forward in a practical way.

education.institution: David Eccles School of Business, University of Utah
David Eccles School of Business, University of Utah
education.degree: Master of Science in Information Systems
Bachelor of Arts in Sociology, Minor in Economics

roles.title: Associate Vice President, Institutional Effectiveness
roles.description: Lead a portfolio of analytics, data science, and strategic enablement teams. Oversee enterprise data infrastructure, governance protocols, and performance systems to support evidence-based planning, policy, and decision-making. Partner with senior leadership across academic and administrative units to embed analytics into institutional strategy and transformation.
bullets: Created and launched an AI project viability and implementation roadmap to expand analytical capabilities across the institution.
Developed and institutionalized a standardized metrics framework to drive KPI alignment and executive decision-making.
Led analytics infrastructure transformation from legacy tools to an Azure-based platform, modernizing pipelines and enhancing data access.
Rolled out Agile-based intake and prioritization systems to improve BI development velocity and reduce backlog.
Strengthened governance, metadata management, and data security protocols across the analytics lifecycle.
Delivered executive-facing insights and long-range forecasting through automated dashboards and predictive models.
Cultivated a data-literate culture by supporting training, end-user enablement, and cross-functional collaboration.
Developed a Data Governance framework to institutionalize practices ensuring data security, quality, and accessibility.

roles.title: Director, Data Science and Analytics
roles.description: Led, mentored, and supervised a team of data analysts, data engineers, and researchers. Managed data projects and addressed analytics inquiries from a wide range of stakeholders.
bullets: Implemented a BI modernization roadmap, transitioning from fragmented tools to a centralized Power BI platform integrated with Oracle SQL Server.
Strengthened partnership with college leadership to prioritize and deliver high-impact analytics solutions, including executive dashboards on enrollment, retention, and institutional KPIs.
Improved project intake and prioritization processes for dashboard and reporting requests, driving alignment between analytics capacity and stakeholder needs.
Standardized documentation practices, data definitions, and governance protocols to improve trust and reduce rework.
Deployed data literacy trainings across academic and administrative units, fostering self-service capability.

roles.title: Assistant Director, Business Analytics
roles.description: Led, mentored, and supervised a team of data analysts. Managed data projects and responded to ad-hoc data requests from diverse stakeholders.
bullets: Improved ad-hoc data requests intake process with Agile methods and prioritization frameworks — minimized response times and reduced data quality errors.
Created compliance dashboards and institutional risk reports for accreditation and internal audit reviews.

roles.title: Business Intelligence Administrator
roles.description: Established robust data governance practices and effectively communicated data quality and management processes across the department. Developed and delivered data products that empowered informed decision-making.
bullets: Developed and implemented a comprehensive Data Governance strategy, including forming a dedicated committee to ensure effective data management practices.
Established data quality standards and communication protocols across the department.

roles.title: Information Technology and Data Analytics, Director
roles.description: Oversaw information technology and data analytics operations. Collaborated closely with leadership and stakeholders on strategic planning and optimization of technology resources.
bullets: Successfully managed and implemented critical technology improvements, enhancing operational efficiency and productivity.
Conducted user training programs to optimize technology utilization and improve overall productivity.
Developed and executed an analytics and information strategy aligned with the organization's strategic plan, enabling data-driven decision-making.

roles.title: Program Outcomes Manager
roles.description: Oversaw evaluation and measurement of program effectiveness. Developed assessment tools and methodologies to track program outcomes.
bullets: Developed and implemented assessment tools and methodologies to track program outcomes.
Analyzed data to identify trends and areas for improvement, ensuring alignment with the organization's mission.
Led training sessions to enhance staff understanding of outcome measurement and promote data-driven decision-making.

leadershipSkills: Translating complex technical needs for non-technical stakeholders

selectedWork.title: Analytics Infrastructure Transformation
selectedWork.description: Led the migration of fragmented legacy analytics tools to a unified Azure-based platform at SLCC — modernizing pipelines, improving data access, and enabling a new level of self-service analytics.
outcomes: Migrated institution to Azure-based cloud analytics platform
Reduced time-to-insight for leadership from days to hours

selectedWork.title: Enterprise BI Modernization
selectedWork.description: Replaced a fragmented reporting landscape with a centralized Power BI platform integrated with Oracle SQL Server — giving leadership consistent, trusted data for the first time.
outcomes: Centralized BI onto a single Power BI platform
Built executive dashboards tracking enrollment, retention, and KPIs
Standardized data definitions across all reporting

selectedWork.title: AI Viability & Implementation Roadmap
selectedWork.description: Created and launched an institution-wide AI project viability framework — enabling leadership to evaluate, prioritize, and responsibly implement AI tools across academic and administrative functions.
outcomes: Built first AI evaluation and governance framework
Aligned AI adoption to institutional strategic priorities
Reduced risk of ad-hoc AI tool proliferation

selectedWork.title: Data Governance Framework
selectedWork.description: Designed and institutionalized a comprehensive Data Governance program — establishing standards for data security, quality, metadata management, and accountability across the analytics lifecycle.
outcomes: Established data ownership and stewardship roles
Improved data quality and reduced rework significantly
Created documentation and metadata standards organization-wide

selectedWork.title: Standardized Metrics & KPI Framework
selectedWork.description: Replaced a bloated set of disconnected metrics with a lean, meaningful framework that showed what was actually changing — and drove real alignment at the executive level.
outcomes: Aligned institutional KPIs to strategic priorities
Created a metrics framework used across leadership planning
Built a culture of honest, evidence-based assessment

selectedWork.title: Agile Analytics Operations
selectedWork.description: Introduced Agile-based intake and prioritization systems to modernize how the analytics team operated — reducing backlog, improving delivery speed, and building stakeholder trust.
outcomes: Reduced analytics request backlog by over 40%
Improved stakeholder satisfaction with delivery timelines
Created transparent prioritization visible to all teams

additional: Adjunct Instructor — University of Utah, David Eccles School of Business
Certificate: Data Strategy — Leveraging Data as a Competitive Advantage (Berkeley Haas)

pillars.title: Strategy
pillars.description: I help teams set direction and focus on what matters. That means cutting through noise, aligning around priorities, and building plans that can actually be followed.

pillars.title: Data & Insight
pillars.description: I help people use data to make better decisions — not just report numbers, but build understanding. I turn analysis into stories that drive action.

pillars.title: Systems & Change
pillars.description: I help organizations improve how work gets done — designing better infrastructure, fixing broken flows, and leading the people side of change with care.

pillars.title: Leadership & Learning
pillars.description: I build teams, guide people through hard transitions, and create the conditions for real growth. I care about how people experience their work, not just their outputs.
